SYSTEM_PROMPT: A cinematic winter portrait world inspired by analog flash photography captured during snowy nights. Scenes are set in quiet winter forests or snow-covered outdoor environments illuminated primarily by direct on-camera flash, creating a raw editorial aesthetic reminiscent of early 2000s fashion photography and underground film photography. Subjects are often photographed holding vintage cameras, interacting with the lens as if capturing the viewer. Bright flash illumination contrasts against deep, cold night shadows, producing high-contrast lighting with strong specular highlights on skin and snow particles. Snowfall appears frozen in mid-air by the flash, creating sparkling textures and atmospheric depth throughout the frame. Color grading leans toward cold cyan, teal, and icy blue tones with subtle analog film grain and slight color bloom around light sources. Wardrobe emphasizes winter fashion elements such as fur hats, oversized sweaters, dark coats, and textured fabrics that absorb the cold ambient light while reflecting the camera flash. The atmosphere feels intimate, spontaneous, and documentary-like, as if capturing fleeting moments during a winter night walk. The result evokes nostalgic analog photography aesthetics, blending cinematic winter environments with direct flash portraiture, tactile textures, and emotionally candid expressions.
BACKGROUNDS: snowfall drifting through cold night air
shadowy pine trees in winter darkness
quiet snowy clearing illuminated by flash
POSES: holding a vintage camera toward the viewer
lifting the camera as if about to take a photo
looking directly into the lens while holding the camera
camera held close to the face
casual portrait stance in falling snow
